title: chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. latifolius
intro: Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. latifolius, also known as the broad-leaved rabbitbrush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. latifolius include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infused oils, allowing for various uses in traditional medicine, such as topical applications and internal remedies.
